description: Project-specific configuration for this instance of the process framework. Update this file when adapting framework to a new project.

project.name: LinkWatcher
project.description: Real-time link maintenance system using file system watching

paths.description: Project-specific directory paths relative to root or absolute

testing.description: Test runner configuration — language-specific commands are in process-framework/languages-config/{language}/{language}-config.json

integration.description: Integration with project-specific tools and systems

layering_rules.description: Per-project layer dependency rules. Source of truth for layer-boundary checks at PF-TSK-032 Code Quality Standards Validation. See process-framework/guides/00-setup/source-code-layout-guide.md for narrative documentation and process-framework/guides/05-validation/feature-validation-guide.md (Layer-Boundary Validation) for usage. Empty 'layers' = no enforcement (backward-compatible default). Populate during PF-TSK-064/066 onboarding or after ADRs codify layer-boundary invariants.

_layer_schema_example._comment: Reference schema for a populated layer entry. Remove the leading underscore on the key when adopting. 'name' is the layer's identifier referenced by 'may_import_from' in other layers; 'directory_glob' identifies source files belonging to this layer; 'may_import_from' is the allowlist (importing from a layer not listed here is a finding).
_layer_schema_example.name: ui

cross_feature_isolation.description: If true, services in one feature may not import non-service layers from another feature directly. Forces service-to-service composition across feature boundaries. Independent of 'layers' — can be enabled separately.
